summary:
Human dentition features specialized tooth structures including incisors for cutting, canines for tearing, and molars for grinding, reflecting adaptations to process both plant and animal-based foods. Evidence from Paleolithic diets supports an ancestral omnivorous eating pattern, validating the classification of human dentition as omnivorous.  

\boxed{B}
